claims.claim: Exact finite tie-aware Best-of-N laws predict selected utility on finite graph-physics candidate pools.
In controlled graph-physics toy worlds, high-N raw selection can increase internal score while selected real utility stagnates or falls.
Graph-energy calibration and constraint probing improve selected-tail utility in the controlled synthetic setting.
The method is validated on real robot systems.
The method establishes broad physics benchmark superiority or state-of-the-art performance.
claims.evidence: no broad benchmark suite is present
